competencies: Distinguish relational and non-relational database models
Design databases using object-oriented and semi-structured models
Apply non-relational DBMS capabilities to applications
Formulate queries with OQL, XPath/XQuery and SQL:2003
Develop applications on non-relational databases
description_en: Advanced study of non-relational database models, their concepts, query languages and application development on non-relational DBMS.

competencies: Apply IR, NLP and HCI approaches in practice
Conduct a state-of-the-art literature review
Write a scientific paper on an information-organization topic
Present and defend a hands-on research project
description_en: Seminar where students write a scientific paper and present a hands-on project in information retrieval, NLP or HCI.

competencies: Assess state-of-the-art solutions in database research
Apply advanced database implementation techniques
Work with main-memory database algorithms
Apply storage and indexing techniques for NoSQL systems
description_en: Advanced, research-driven course on current database topics such as main-memory algorithms, heterogeneous processing and NoSQL storage techniques.

competencies: Read and analyze current deep learning research papers
Apply deep learning to a specific application domain
Build domain-specific deep learning pipelines
Work on server infrastructure in a research team
Present scientific results in written and oral form
description_en: Advanced seminar applying current deep learning research to build domain-specific pipelines and present scientific results in teams.

competencies: Explain advanced networking concepts to others
Apply overlay and peer-to-peer networking techniques
Understand distributed hash tables and blockchains
Reason about congestion control protocols like QUIC
Analyze secure network architectures
description_en: Advanced networking course covering overlay networks, distributed hash tables, blockchains, smart contracts and modern congestion control.

competencies: Collaborate in teams to solve technical challenges
Manage a project and define goals and milestones
Acquire domain knowledge to derive solutions
Document and present project work and achievements
description_en: Team and individual lab projects on research topics such as robotics, sensor data processing, navigation and industrial machine learning.

competencies: Model and simulate Markov and non-Markovian stochastic processes
Apply solution methods for Markov chains
Work with hidden Markovian and non-Markovian models
Implement and program stochastic model classes
Solve modelling problems from medicine and engineering
description_en: Course on discrete stochastic modelling with Markov chains, hidden models and proxel simulation applied to medicine and engineering problems.

competencies: Apply Clean Code Development principles and practices
Select and adapt suitable software concepts for a project
Use modern software engineering tools and techniques
Distribute tasks and collaborate in a development team
description_en: Advanced software engineering course teaching Clean Code Development principles, modern tools and collaborative development practices.

competencies: Explain core concepts of computer graphics
Implement a simple rendering program
Apply transformations, clipping and rasterization
Implement lighting, texturing and ray tracing techniques
description_en: Introductory computer graphics course covering modeling, transformations, rasterization, lighting, texturing and ray tracing with programming tasks.

competencies: Design relational and non-relational databases for engineering
Assess engineering data management requirements
Apply product data management with database systems
Support workflows, interoperability and product lifecycle management
description_en: Course on database-backed data management for engineering applications, covering product data management, interoperability and product lifecycle support.

competencies: Apply supervised classification algorithms to tabular data
Apply unsupervised clustering algorithms
Evaluate and compare data mining models
Perform data engineering to prepare data for mining
Build workflows for model evaluation
description_en: Introduction to data mining covering supervised classification, unsupervised clustering, model evaluation and data engineering fundamentals.

competencies: Design learning algorithms for temporal data and data streams
Apply supervised and unsupervised stream learning methods
Detect and handle concept drift
Perform continuous evaluation of temporal models
Interpret models induced from temporal data
description_en: Advanced data mining course on temporal data and streams, covering stream clustering, classification, concept drift and continuous evaluation.

competencies: Import and preprocess raw data from files, databases and web APIs
Transform data for modelling in Python
Perform exploratory data analysis with statistics and visualization
Build and evaluate predictive models in Python
description_en: Hands-on course teaching data science with Python: importing, transforming, exploring and modelling real-world data for prediction and insight.

competencies: Model relational databases from practical scenarios
Map ER schemas to relational models
Use relational algebra and SQL
Apply normalization and formal design criteria
Develop database applications with views and triggers
description_en: Foundational database course covering relational modelling, ER mapping, SQL, normalization and database application programming.

competencies: Work collaboratively in interdisciplinary project teams
Carry out scientific work and contribute to publications
Plan and manage an innovative engineering task
Document and present project results
description_en: Interdisciplinary team project where students collaborate on an innovative digital engineering task and produce a report and presentation.

competencies: Implement distributed and parallel databases
Design data distribution across nodes
Process queries and transactions in distributed systems
Optimize runtime performance, reliability and availability
Apply transactional replication techniques
description_en: Course on distributed and parallel data management covering distribution design, distributed query and transaction processing, and replication.

competencies: Develop kinematic and discrete-time prediction models for mobile robots
Apply least-squares methods for localization and tracking
Design and apply Kalman filters and nonlinear generalizations
Perform dynamic state estimation for mobile systems
description_en: Course on state estimation for autonomous mobile robots, covering kinematic models, least-squares localization, tracking and Kalman filtering.

competencies: Organize scientific projects in human-computer interaction
Apply eudaimonic and hedonic design principles
Evaluate ethical issues and dark patterns in interface design
Conduct well-being-focused system development
Write and review scientific papers
description_en: Seminar on eudaimonic, well-being-focused interaction design covering ethical interface design, dark patterns and scientific writing in HCI.

competencies: Apply mathematical principles of vector and tensor fields
Acquire and process flow data
Create direct, texture-based and geometry-based flow visualizations
Apply feature-based and topological flow visualization methods
description_en: Course on flow visualization covering vector and tensor fields and direct, texture-based, geometry-based, feature-based and topological methods.

competencies: Design data structures for geometric problems
Apply balanced and self-organizing search trees
Perform amortized analysis of data structures
Use interval, range, segment and quad-trees
Build point-location and persistent data structures
description_en: Course on designing, analyzing and applying geometric data structures such as range trees, quad-trees and point-location structures.

competencies: Conduct systematic research literature reviews
Write and review scientific papers
Apply human-centred approaches and technologies
Evaluate research papers and peer work
Deliver a conference-style presentation
description_en: Seminar/project on human-centred approaches and technologies emphasizing scientific writing, literature reviews and the paper review process.

competencies: Design safe, efficient and human-centred work environments
Apply Human-Centred Design and Manufacturing principles
Analyze physical and cognitive ergonomics
Apply risk assessment methodologies
Evaluate human-robot collaboration in Industry 4.0
description_en: Course on human-centred manufacturing and usability covering ergonomics, risk assessment and human integration into Industry 4.0 processes.

competencies: Critically evaluate state-of-the-art language models
Explain language representation, tokenization and transformers
Design human-centred NLP approaches
Build NLP solutions using large language models
Work with multimodal AI architectures
description_en: Course on human-centred natural language processing covering tokenization, transformers, large language models and multimodal AI architectures.

competencies: Apply in-memory data management technologies
Understand cloud computing technologies for enterprise systems
Adapt data science methods to novel problems
Contribute digitalization solutions in interdisciplinary projects
description_en: Two-semester course on in-memory and cloud technologies for enterprise and information systems, bridging computer science and engineering domains.

competencies: Work independently on a scientific question
Acquire in-depth knowledge in a specialized field
Present and interpret results in oral and written form
Assess the significance of research results
description_en: Individual scientific project where a student independently investigates an agreed research question under supervision and presents the results.

competencies: Program in an imperative, object-oriented language
Reason about algorithm design and runtime
Apply fundamental data structures such as trees and hash tables
Implement sorting and searching algorithms
Analyze algorithm complexity with Big-O notation
description_en: Introductory computer science course for engineers covering programming, fundamental data structures, algorithms and complexity analysis.

competencies: Design distributed sensor systems and networks
Process data across networks of sensors
Model measurement noise and errors
Apply distributed sensor data fusion methods
Compare and evaluate information-processing approaches
description_en: Introduction to distributed sensor data fusion covering networked sensor systems, error modelling and methods for fusing sensor information.

competencies: Design and develop data warehouse applications
Apply the Extract-Transform-Load process
Build OLAP and multidimensional data models
Use data-warehouse-specific DBMS functionality
Optimize query execution for data warehouses
description_en: Introduction to data warehousing covering ETL, OLAP, the multidimensional model, indexing structures and business intelligence applications.

competencies: Apply deep learning techniques to solve given problems
Build network architectures such as CNNs, RNNs and Transformers
Apply regularization and optimization techniques
Design and conduct deep learning experiments
Document work for reproducibility and transparency
description_en: Introduction to deep learning covering neural network fundamentals, CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, regularization and experiment design.

competencies: Analyze discrete, continuous and hybrid systems with simulation tools
Integrate initial value problems numerically
Carry out input and output analysis for stochastic models
Select an appropriate modelling paradigm
Perform and interpret a simulation study
description_en: Introduction to simulation covering discrete-event, continuous and hybrid systems, numerical integration, stochastic models and simulation studies.

competencies: Apply generative models to solve given problems
Train energy-based and autoregressive models
Build GANs and variational learning models
Apply flow-based and diffusion models
Design and conduct experiments with generative models
description_en: Advanced course on learning generative models including energy-based models, autoregressive models, GANs, flow-based and diffusion models.

competencies: Analyze typical failure modes of ML systems
Select test and evaluation methods for safety-critical ML
Assess uncertainty estimation, anomaly detection and robustness
Understand adversarial threat models and defenses
Reflect on regulatory and ethical conditions for ML deployment
description_en: Introduction to machine learning safety covering failure modes, uncertainty, anomaly detection, adversarial threats and regulatory considerations.

competencies: Create machine learning pipelines and algorithms
Implement decision trees, perceptrons and KNN classifiers
Apply Bayesian and instance-based learning
Perform cluster analysis and association learning
Evaluate predictive performance of classifiers
description_en: Core machine learning course covering concept learning, decision trees, Bayesian and neural methods, clustering and hypothesis evaluation.
